sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Terminology 7:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Terminology 7:1
items.html: Malchut is called Et [comprised of Aleph and Tav]. It implies that it contains the alphabet from Aleph to Tav. It is so because Malchut is the root of the twenty-two letters, and for this reason, the vessels are called letters. (Item 30, and Inner Light)
